riddle:
Here is the revised riddle:

1. Seek the famed US carmaker associated with the founder who introduced the moving assembly line; from its summary box, what year marks when the company first opened its doors? After noting that year, follow the page link labeled with the common plural for motor vehicles to continue.

2. There exists an article reached by that link which redirects from the generic name for personal motor cars; on that page, under the heading that groups parts and styling of a roadgoing vehicle, how many subsections are listed in the table of contents? Use the site search if you need to re-find this article.

3. What single digit do you get if you take the number found in clue 2 and repeatedly sum its digits until only one digit remains (the iterated digit-sum or digital root)?

4. If you visit the article about the Midwestern city tied to early mass auto manufacture and social history, read the side summary and report how many feet/meters above sea level its elevation is given as.

5. The article whose opening line names the highest class of single-seater circuit racing hides a simple structural count — how many top-level (main) sections appear in its table of contents?

6. In a short account of the British racing team famous for its orange livery, look to the line of links that point to other-language versions of the page; how many language links are shown there?

7. The corporate chimera created by the 2021 merging of two European auto groups trades under ticker STLA; what was its closing share price on 15 January 2021? (Think of this as the market figure you must read from historical quotes.)

8. (A quick transformation) Advance the market figure you obtained in clue 7 to the next prime number greater than it; what is that prime?

9. Pinpoint the slender, triangular skyscraper's coordinates in Manhattan (about 40.74111, −73.98972) and count how many universities or higher-education institutions are located near that point.

10. Finally, compute the following to produce a single digit (0–9): take the absolute difference between clue 1 and clue 5, then reduce that difference modulo clue 6. In symbolic form: abs(clue 1 - clue 5) % clue 6. The result is your final digit.

metadata.theme: From Ford to the world stage: how the automobile reshaped industry, cities, racing and modern auto conglomerates
